title: View DDL Parsing
description: ANTLR listener actors parse SQL VIEW definitions (CREATE/ALTER/DROP) and extract column metadata from SELECT clauses.

actors.name: ANTLRParserEngine
actors.description: ANTLR v4 runtime that walks the parse tree and fires enter/exit events to registered listeners

actors.name: MySqlAntlrDdlParser
actors.description: Central parser coordinating listeners; holds skipViews flag, databaseTables registry, and signals view lifecycle events

actors.name: DatabaseTables
actors.description: In-memory table/view schema registry; supports editTable, editOrCreateTable, overwriteTable, removeTable, forTable

actors.name: DDLEventConsumer
actors.description: Downstream consumer receiving signalCreateView / signalAlterView / signalDropView notifications

use_cases.name: Parse CREATE VIEW
steps: Check parser.skipViews(); skip entire body if true
If uidList present: add named columns via Column.editor().name(columnName).create()
Instantiate ViewSelectedColumnsParserListener(tableEditor, parser) and add to listeners
Set default charset if absent via parser.charsetForTable(tableEditor.tableId())

use_cases.name: Parse ALTER VIEW
steps: Check parser.skipViews(); skip body if true
editTable(parseQualifiedTableId(ctx.fullId())) → throws ParsingException if view does not exist
If uidList present: add fresh named columns
Instantiate ViewSelectedColumnsParserListener and add to listeners
Set default charset if absent via parser.currentDatabaseCharset()
use_cases.error_path: ParsingException if target view does not exist in databaseTables

use_cases.name: Parse DROP VIEW
steps: For each fullId in ctx: parseQualifiedTableId → tableId

use_cases.name: Extract VIEW Selected Columns
use_cases.description: Sub-listener dynamically added by Create/AlterViewParserListener to resolve columns from the VIEW's SELECT body
internal_steps: exitAtomTableItem: parseQualifiedTableId → lookup table in databaseTables or tableByAlias; store alias mapping
exitSubqueryTableItem: parse alias uid → store selectTableEditor snapshot under alias
parseSelectElements: handle SELECT * (all cols from all aliased tables), SELECT tbl.* (all cols from one table), SELECT col [AS alias] (single column with optional rename)
Column resolution: match column by name from selectedTable, rename if alias present
data_structures: tableByAlias: Map<TableId, Table> — alias-to-table mapping cleared after each query spec

use_cases.name: Skip View Processing
use_cases.description: All three DDL listeners check parser.skipViews() flag; if true they skip schema mutation but CREATE/ALTER still signal the event
use_cases.note: DropViewParserListener skips signalling when skipViews=true; Create/AlterView always signal regardless

use_cases.name: Signal View Lifecycle Event
methods: signalAlterView(TableId, TableId previousId, ParserRuleContext) → signalAlterView(id, previousId, getText(ctx))

key_relationships: CreateViewParserListener depends_on MySqlAntlrDdlParser (parser, skipViews, databaseTables, signalCreateView)
AlterViewParserListener depends_on MySqlAntlrDdlParser (parser, skipViews, databaseTables, signalAlterView)
DropViewParserListener depends_on MySqlAntlrDdlParser (parser, skipViews, databaseTables, signalDropView)
MySqlAntlrDdlParserListener registers all three top-level listeners at initialization (lines 65-67)
ViewSelectedColumnsParserListener produces List<Column> consumed by Create/AlterViewParserListener

dynamic_listeners: ViewSelectedColumnsParserListener (added on enterCreate/AlterView, removed on exitCreate/AlterView)

important_notes: Both MySQL and MariaDB have parallel implementations (connector-mysql vs connector-mariadb packages) with identical structure
ViewSelectedColumnsParserListener is transient — lives only during CREATE/ALTER VIEW parsing
ALTER VIEW throws ParsingException if target view does not pre-exist in databaseTables
DROP VIEW iterates a list: single statement can drop multiple views
signalAlterView always fires (even when skipViews=true) so downstream consumers stay in sync
Column aliasing is fully resolved: SELECT col AS alias maps the column under the alias name in the view schema
